The Fab 5, The Traffic Light  & The No Ghosts Shall PAS



Ry Schwartz & Copyhackers

2020



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #5

3 Post-Webinar Templates for 3 Key Segments

What they need:

Action items:

Themeplate (The Fab 5)

See it in action (10x Freelance Copywriter 2018)

Segment 2: Registrants who didn’t show up (aka ghosted)

What they need:

Action items:

Themeplate: No Ghost Shall PAS

See it in action (Amy Porterfield’s Digital Course Academy - Jan 2019)

Segment 3: Registered but left early

What they need:

Action items:

Themeplate (The Traffic Light)

See it in action (Amy Porterfield’s Digital Course Academy - Jan 2019)



3 Post-Webinar Templates for 3 Key Segments 

Hopefully you’re using (or considering using) webinars in your digital marketing. It’s one of the most effective conversion channels for nearly any kind of product or service (online courses, consulting programs, masterminds, software, etc.) 



However, one of the biggest challenges in webinar marketing is getting registrants to show up live where they have the best chance to convert. 



A well-written webinar “show up sequence”  (like the templates taught in 10x Launches/10x Emails) can more than DOUBLE your showup rate. But regardless of the uplift, you’ll always be left with 3 distinct audience segments following your webinar.



Segment 1: Registrants who stayed till the end (but didn’t buy) 

Segment 2: Registrants who didn’t show up at all (ghosted)

Segment 3: Registrants who showed up but dropped off early (aka didn’t get the full pitch) 



Each prospect has been “coached” to a certain degree to the final conversion. 



The prospect who stayed till the end has already been exposed to all of your sales arguments (emotional and logical) and therefore requires a MUCH different follow up conversation than the prospect who didn’t show up at all or the prospect who left early. 



This 3-part set of email templates is used to engage each of these segments in the most appropriate immediate* post-webinar conversation that hits on the points most likely to convert them into a buyer (or keep them in the “buying” pocket)  



*when I say “immediate”, I really do mean immediate. These 3 templates  are designed to be sent RIGHT after the webinar ends. 



Unless you’re testing a hypothesis (or have a good reason to do otherwise) these 3 segments will often converge in the sales emails and closing emails. 

Segment 1: Showed up live (but didn’t buy):

What they need: 

Urgency (a reason to act now)

Courage/Confidence (not more information)

Exclusive empowerment (which breeds confidence)



In short, they need urgency (not information) and an exclusively empowered close:



They also don’t need more “selling” of your core product.  They’ve already shown high-commitment and high-intent by staying till the end. 



They’ve consumed your entire sales message and all the logical/emotional reasons why they should buy. 



Repetition (or new presentations via stories/case studies) of these sales arguments can come later in your sequence, but right now they need a confident nudge. 



Most importantly, they need to feel rewarded for staying till the end -- and empowered to act on the emotions/ideas/future-paced reality that you just created for them. 



There’s a short window where they remain present to all those emotional and mental dynamics you created for them (and new reality you future paced) -- and you want to do everything you can to capitalize on that  precious window. 

Action items: 

Have a super valuable “stayed till the end’ bonus for a max of 24 hours (we use this at CH) 

Send the “Fab 5” right after the webinar ends 

Themeplate (The Fab 5) 



Step 1: WTSAY Opening 

About who they are and/or what they want (or don’t want) 

About getting your help to create that outcome

About getting access to the *for your eyes only* bonus (exclusive empowerment) 

Step 2: Sell the value and benefits of the bonus

Step 3: CTA framed around the bonus specifically

Step 4: Confident close around why you want them to get the bonus

Step 5: Final CTA (pushing the urgency/deadline) 



See it in action (10x Freelance Copywriter 2018) 



Subject:

The link we just shared (and your secret 10xFC bonus)



Body:



Allow me begin by offering you the highest of fives. 







You stayed till the end. 



Which means 3 things.



You’re a hella ambitious freelance copywriter who’s ready to make this your year. No more excuses. No more time spent “on the verge”. You’re gonna do it -- and you’re gonna be freakin’ happy and proud you did. 



You want the training, coaching and accountability to smash through your plateaus, slash your learning curve and enjoy an accelerated ascent to the top of the freelance copy game. 



And finally… 



You’ve earned your access to the secret “because you stayed till the end bonus” that no one else will ever know existed.



We’re talkin, of course, about the epic 5-hour proposal bootcamp.



You’ll spend a full day with Amy Posner and moi (who’ve delivered a combined 500+ proposals with a dollar value well north of 7 figures) where we’ll lead you through everything you need to nail down your winning proposal… and most importantly, get more “yes’s”. 



You’ll leave this online bootcamp knowing… 



How to master the art of the pre-proposal call (and how to prime the pump for the eventual “close”)

How to present your offer with Clint-Eastwood level cool even if you just dropped a price 5x higher than anything you’ve ever quoted before.

How (and when) to follow up… and how to close the deal without being weird, desperate or pushy.

How to ensure you start the work on time and get paid on time (aka how to operate like a Madison Avenue agency even if you’re a solo act) 



Get your private invite to this closed-door online bootcamp when you join from this link TODAY



Let me be super blunt..  



If you leave this workshop having your proposal nailed down (from what it looks like, to how to present it for the “yes”)... 



… this alone can 10x your investment in the entire program even if you never log into module 1 or join the slack channel. 



Nailing your proposal is that powerful in 10x’ing your freelance career. 



And because I really, reaaaaaally give a sh*t about you having a record-breaking year in 2019.



I really want you to have it.



But I want you to want to have it too. 



So meet me halfway and get it here before that crappy timer gets a say in how/when you grow your freelance career.



-jo



<COUNTDOWN TIMER> 



Segment 2: Registrants who didn’t show up (aka ghosted) 

A few years ago we released a “webinar show up” template called the “Ghostbuster” to help dissuade registrants from missing the live webinar. 



While this template is effective in bumping up show up rates, it goes without saying that you’ll nearly always have more registrants ghost than show up (a 50% show up rate would be WAY above average in most industries). 



This leaves us with a relatively large (and diverse) segment to market to in the immediate  follow up as they had high intent ( they signed up), but presumably low commitment. 



The goal of this immediate post-webinar follow up is to diagnose the no-show, reflect back and amplify their  initial motivation/intent/commitment, and give clear guidance to get back on track. 

What they need: 

Empathy around the no-show (not guilt/shame or heavy FOMO) 

To NOT feel like they “missed the boat”

Clarity on how to get back on track

Reinforcement of the initial intent/motivation for signing up. More specifically, the initial intentions that a PERFECT buyer would’ve had. 

Action items: 

Create a webinar replay page

Offer a follow up (encore) presentation 2 or 3 days later

Themeplate: No Ghost Shall PAS



This template follows a pretty typical “PASO” formula. In this case, we treat “missing the webinar” as the pain/problem, we agitate by listing the reasons (and cost) of missing it, and then we offer the solution in the form of the replay and encore presentation. 



P: Directly call out the fact that they missed the webinar



A: Diagnose and slightly agitate the logical reasons why they missed it (the key to doing this effectively is to deflect the blame to “external causes” -- and show them that you’re on their side). Your should err on a playful tone (gifs, emojis, or simply writing with empathy) -- and NEVER use an accusatory/blaming/shaming tone. 



Bonus Step: Wherever possible, try to mirror the outcome that your product helps create with the reasons they missed it. (this works best when your product helps save/organize time) 



S/O: This is where you give a clear path to solving that pain via the webinar replay OR the next live presentation. You want to create a binary choice between TWO favorable outcomes -- rather than the choice being between “watch the replay” or “do nothing”. There’s also a “convenience anchoring” effect at play that you’ll see in the example. 

See it in action (Amy Porterfield’s Digital Course Academy - Jan 2019) 

* if you’re reading this in January 2019 and  want to see/study this funnel at play (webinar registration page, emails, long form sales page https://www.amyporterfield.com/masterclass/



Subject:

what happened?

what happened? (2 options)



Body:



Ahhh! 



You were *this* close to joining me on our live masterclass together. 







But it looks like something got in the way. 



🤷‍  maybe that ultra-demanding boss (or client) threw a last minute curveball at you?



🤷‍ maybe you couldn’t help but bust your two-episode limit of Riverdale (no spoilers please) 



🤷‍ maybe the kids (the human or furry kind) needed some extra love today. 



Whatever happened… we missed you :-( 



And more importantly, you missed the kind of training that could TOTALLY help you avoid those situations from happening in the future.



Because when you successfully build and launch a profitable digital course that uncouples you from the time for money trap…



🙆🏻 you can afford to take more time off with the fam.



🙆🏻 you can afford to ONLY work with clients you love (and who respect your time)



🙆🏻 you can afford to indulge in that extra guilt-free episode of whatever takes up space on your “currently watching” list. 



So yea… there’s *quite* a bit on the line here. And if it’s cool with you, I’d love to give us a second chance to make it happen.



Option 1: Watch the replay (available now)



Got some time right now and want to dive in before something else claims real estate on your iCal? 



I prep’d a replay for you right here. 



Option 2: Register for one of the few remaining live presentations: 



In a perfect world, you’d join me here. There’s something *magical* that happens when you join hundreds of your peers LIVE and move forward together towards a common goal.



Click here to join the next LIVE masterclass



Either way, please make it a priority to watch this masterclass at some point over the next 24 hours.



I totally get what it feels like to be “crazy busy” and “super overwhelmed”. 



But at the same time, building a digital course business like I’ll teach you on this masterclass is the way to untangle you from that mess. 



If you’re ready to take a stand for yourself, your schedule, and your sanity…



I can’t wait to see you on the next live training (or replay),



Amy



Segment 3: Registered but left early

This is the most “general” or nonspecific of the 3 segments. It’s essentially anyone who showed up but didn’t stay. You’re not exactly sure how much of the webinar or offer they consumed -- and therefore have varying degrees of intent.  



Whereas the actions (or non-actions) of the first two segments are more clear and telling, this segment needs to speak to a broader spectrum of intent. 



We use the “traffic light” system as an analogy for their readiness to buy -- and offer 3 separate CTA’s that match those levels of intent. 

What they need: 

Intent-Appropriate CTA’s

Crystal clear options and guidance (aka next steps)

The ability to self-segment and re-orient and re-engage in your launch 



Action items: 

Create a webinar replay page

Ideally have a long form sales page (or at the very least a checkout page)

Create an opt-out landing page

Themeplate (The Traffic Light) 



Step 1: Reflect back the fact that they were on the webinar which reinforces commitment

Step 2: Immediately pivot to offering clear direction and next steps

Step 3: The traffic light segmentation system:

Green light for people who nearly stayed till the end and are “almost there” (sales page)

Yellow light for people who either didn’t watch much of it or are still low-intent (replay)

Red light: for people who have no interest regardless of how much they watched (unsub)

Step 4: Insist that they take one of those actions (to re-engage/re-commit) 



See it in action (Amy Porterfield’s Digital Course Academy - Jan 2019) 



Subject:

[DCA] Your important next steps… 

Green light… yellow  light… red light… 

“cool… but what’s next, Amy?”  



Body:



You did it!



Thanks SO much for joining me on our recent masterclass: The 3 Behind-the-Scenes Secrets to Digital Course Success in 2019 and Beyond.



This training has literally been YEARS in the making, and I’m so grateful I could share it with incredible, soon-to-be digital course creators like you!



So now you might be wondering… “cool! but what’s next, Amy?” 



And here’s the thing…



It all depends on you!



💚 Option 1: If you’re entirely committed to creating and launching your profitable digital course business THIS year, then I’d love nothing more than to meet you inside Module 1 (and the private fb group) in the next few mins. Yes, this special “launch promo” will be running for a few more days… but if you already know it’s time for you to create and launch your course, then why waste another moment “thinking about it” when you can start crossing exciting ‘to do’s” off your course creation project list? 



=> Here’s the link that will get you up and running in a matter of minutes



💛 Option 2: It sounds exciting but you’re still in “think about it” mode. And I get it… while the financial investment is super affordable for this type of program (not to mention risk-free)... we will be asking you to invest time, energy and focus in creating and launching your course over the next few months. This might be a pretty big departure from your normal “day to day” routine. And even though the prospect of finally separating yourself from the time for money trap is exciting… you still need a bit more time to decide if you’re ready to take that leap.



If that’s you… I’d love it if you could re-watch the masterclass. 



Except instead of taking notes, just see if you can picture yourself doing the things we talk about (recording your course, launching with webinars, etc.) If things resonate and you feel a big “yes” rumbling in your gut, than I’d love to welcome you inside Digital Course Academy later today. 



Finally...



💔 Option 3: You enjoyed the masterclass, but you’re absolutely sure that you don’t want to create a digital course this year. Maybe it’s not the right time… maybe you’re totally cool with the way things already are in your business and have a set plan for 2019. Either way, you’d prefer NOT to hear more about Digital Course Academy so that you can stay fully focused on your existing plan for 2019.  If that’s the case…



=> Here’s the link to unsubscribe from all Digital Course Academy updates, bonuses and discounts. 



That’s all I got for you today!



Whatever you decide, I highly encourage you to dig deep and make a decision today. 



The ROI on sitting on the fence is nil -- the ROI on powerfully moving forward with whatever feels most right can be life-changing.



ALL my best,



Amy
